name: showcase2
_comment: Slice AW second material — a DIFFERENT node mix from showcase.mat.json to demonstrate the authoring range. baseColor = Multiply(TextureSample(checker), Constant(tint)) so the checker texture is tinted by a constant (float4*float4 -> narrowed to .xyz); roughness = Fresnel(power=2) so the surface gets rougher at grazing angles; metallic = Constant(0.8) (a near-metal). NOTE on the type system: the PBROutput scalar ports (metallic/roughness) require a FLOAT source — a TextureSample is float4, so 'metallic from a TextureSample' is not expressible without a scalar-extract/swizzle node (out of scope: no node types beyond AV's set). We therefore drive baseColor via a Multiply that CONSUMES the TextureSample, roughness via Fresnel, and metallic via a Constant — a genuinely different graph that validates. On Vulkan this renders via the RUNTIME compile path (codegen -> dxc subprocess -> SPIR-V); on Metal via the committed build-time codegen (shaders/generated/mat_showcase2.frag.hlsl).
